sessions.intent: Pressure-test the comparison redesign against the real branch code and write the settled architect plan.

before.assumptions: The coder's plan (.bot/compare-redesign/coder/compare-redesign-plan.md) framed compare as owned-per-type, async, no Type.Name switch. Read the real code to test the framing.
before.risk: Plan rules 1-2 (Value-is-raw, type-references-Data) and rule 8 (async because materialize is I/O) appeared to contradict the on-disk item.@this model; rule 4 (each type owns coercion) risked breaking antisymmetry.

actions.context: Verified the real comparison machinery on compare-redesign (two scout agents had read the recycled prevars-in-pr state by mistake). Confirmed: NormalizeTypes is symmetric; per-type values currently hold their backing (text holds string) and Data.Value mixes raw scalars and wrappers; the type entity already routes name->family behavior via Convert/Conversions; materialize is sync today.

Wrote the settled design after the whiteboard session with Ingi. Key corrections vs the coder plan: coercion by per-type rank (winner coerces loser) to preserve antisymmetry; one async value door await data.Value() with sync .Value removed; sync ordering core with sort two-phased to avoid sync-over-async; enum Incomparable is ordering-only with a null carve-out for equality; dispatch reuses the existing name->family routing rather than a new registry.

after.status: Plan written; awaiting Ingi's comments. No stage/test files per request.
after.notes: Plan supersedes the coder plan's rules 1-2, 4, 8 and adds the single async value door + sync ordering core split.
